prompt: A workflow checkpoints after each step, yet a resumed job still sends a confirmation email twice. What is the most likely root cause and fix?
choices: The crash happened between the email sending and the checkpoint being saved; make the send step idempotent (keyed) so re-running it is a no-op
The checkpoint was saved too often; checkpoint less frequently
The email server was too fast; add an artificial delay before sending
The workflow had too many states; merge them into one state

prompt: Two workers both reload the same checkpoint (version 7) and try to advance the workflow. Which mechanisms correctly ensure only one of them succeeds? (Select all that apply.)
choices: A compare-and-set that applies the transition only if the state version is still 7
A lease/lock that a worker must hold to apply a transition
Letting both workers proceed, since checkpoints make duplication harmless
Storing the checkpoint only in each worker's local memory

prompt: A request can sit in the 'In Review' state indefinitely if the reviewer never responds, and nothing else can move it. What is the design flaw, and the standard remedy?
choices: There is no path from 'In Review' toward a terminal state; add a timeout transition (e.g. to 'Escalated') so the workflow can't hang forever
The state name is too long; rename it to something shorter
The checkpoint is too large; remove the reviewer field
The state machine has too few states; the fix is to add more review states
